message:
fcp-audit: verify_chain_with_clock rejects absurd future timestamps

verify_chain previously detected backward timestamp regressions but
accepted arbitrarily-future occurred_at values. A single forged
future-dated entry (e.g., year 2100) is sufficient to poison
FreshnessLevel / AuditStatus computations permanently, since all
later entries only need to be non-decreasing from that point.

Add pub const MAX_FUTURE_TIMESTAMP_SKEW_SECS = 300 (matches
Kerberos/IAM convention) and a new verify_chain_with_clock that
delegates to verify_chain and then emits audit.timestamp_future
(critical) for any entry beyond now+skew. Use saturating_add on
the ceiling so u64::MAX timestamps don't panic. Callers with no
wall clock (offline replays) keep using verify_chain unchanged.

Closes flywheel_connectors-hhjnp.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
